current.note: The v2.0.4 candidate is prepared in a PR; its tag, GitHub Latest Release, Pages finalization, and Vercel production deployment remain separate approval gates.

fixes.claim: The embedded terminal offers only fixed shell choices, defaults to fish when available, installs fish through a confirmed fixed Homebrew command, and treats user-closed tabs as normal termination.
Application shutdown closes owned bridges, streams, watchers, workers, terminals, and agent child processes without accepting late resources.
Bridge authentication, origin policy, canonical path checks, and request limits fail closed at workspace boundaries.
Concurrent saves, split views, external conflicts, and recoverable-trash failures preserve user drafts and original files.
Release builds produce distinct x64 and arm64 DMGs whose app and native PTY binaries match the declared architecture.
The app selects and downloads only a newer stable release's exact architecture DMG, verifies its size and GitHub SHA-256 digest, and never quits or replaces the app automatically.
Source, packaged app, and mounted x64/arm64 DMG icons retain transparent outer pixels instead of an opaque black square.

features.title: Document workbench
features.claim: Projects, recent documents, the file explorer, open tabs, the document canvas, and tool panes now share one local project workspace.
limitations: This release does not add worktree orchestration or remote project management.

features.title: Format-aware document modes
features.claim: Markdown exposes Source, guarded Rich, and Preview modes; AsciiDoc exposes Source and Preview; JSON exposes Source, Tree, formatting, and parse validation.
limitations: Rich mode is disabled for documents larger than 300,000 characters and for syntax that cannot safely round-trip.
JavaScript, TypeScript, YAML, and plain text remain Source-only.

features.title: Draggable tabs and panes
features.claim: Document tabs and the terminal pane can be placed around the document area with a before-drop spatial preview, resizable splits, and persisted layout state.
limitations: The root workbench currently models one document area and one terminal area rather than an arbitrary pane tree.

features.title: Project login-shell terminal
features.claim: A generic terminal opens the user's default login shell in the project directory, supports multiple sessions, preserves ordered output, and restores a renderer from a bounded screen snapshot while the bridge process is running.
limitations: Shell processes are owned by the app's bridge process and are not guaranteed to survive a full DocPilot restart.
DocPilot does not automatically launch or manage Codex, Claude, or another agent CLI.

features.title: Source and rendered Diff review
features.claim: Source and Preview can display document changes, including a side-by-side option and a Changes rail that navigates between review locations.
limitations: Rendered Diff is a document review surface, not a replacement for a full Git client.

features.title: Project search
features.claim: Command-Shift-F searches file names or local text with include, exclude, case, whole-word, and regular-expression controls and opens a selected result.
limitations: Search reads files in the current local workspace and does not provide remote or indexed code search.

claims.title: Shutdown, security, save integrity, and multi-architecture packaging
claims.claim: Hardened owned-process shutdown, workspace request boundaries, concurrent save integrity, and distinct x64/arm64 release packages.

history.note: This is the verified public baseline immediately before the v2.0.3 update and icon corrections.

claims.title: Preview and launch corrections
claims.claim: Aligned Markdown and AsciiDoc preview typography and overflow, exposed preview controls, followed the system theme, and restored terminal reopening.

history.note: This patch release remains the verified preview and launch behavior baseline for v2.0.2.

claims.title: Document workbench release
claims.claim: Released the document workbench, format-aware modes, pane layout, login-shell terminal, rendered Diff, project search, and the first v2 public manual.

history.note: This is the v2 feature baseline. The bounded feature evidence remains in the features section.

claims.title: AsciiDoc preview support
claims.claim: Added AsciiDoc conversion through a worker, editor highlighting, preview rendering, and corresponding manual guidance.

history.note: The v1.0.28 line contains temporary 1.0.29 version commits that were reverted before the tag; the tagged state is the authority.
This is the earliest repository tag. It is retained as a verified baseline without reconstructing unsupported feature-level history.
